description: research-only kickoff; contract frozen
opening sweep over tight 3x+1 family (44 variants × 10^4 seeds, 153 cycles, 53.6%% reach cycle)
analytic trivial-cycle catalog L≤3 — 116 cycles across 44 variants
conjugacy survey — zero full conjugacies; sub-system embedding via b-scaling; normal form is identity
cycle inheritance tagging — 64 new + 89 inherited; C-007 strong falsified, weak holds; (3,13) carries 9 of 64 new
L>=4 cycle catalog — 37 cycles; (3,13) has 7 cycles at (L=5, t_period=13); empirical period law t_period ≈ L·(1+log2 a) holds within 4%
10x horizon sweep + attractivity probe — C-001 C-003 C-004/C-008 confirmed; C-006 FALSIFIED (543k counterexamples for a∈{5,7} composite b); zero new cycles at 10× horizon
(3,21) low-seed probe — seeds 1..14 all reach a cycle (n=7,14 → b-fixed cycle; rest → L2 cycle starting at 15)
algebraic enumeration of (3,13) L=5 cycles — all 35 compositions yield valid cycles; 35/5 = 7 distinct after rotation dedup; matches sweep 7-for-7
closed-form proof of C-007′ via b-scaling embedding; falsified C-006′ via residue-arithmetic counterexample; promoted refined C-006″ (gcd(a,λ)>1 ⟺ forward-attractive) with 20/20 empirical match
C-011 enumeration — 1,661 (a,b,L,K) tuples in tight scope; 100% predicted/observed match; 41 yield cycles covering 57/153 (37.3%) of atlas
C-011 full coverage check at unbounded (L,K) — all 64 primitive atlas cycles satisfy b|(2^K-a^L); combined with 89 inherited = full closed-form decomposition of 153-cycle atlas
wider-b sweep b∈{23..51} odd — 6M trajectories; C-001 confirmed (3 attempts); C-007′ confirmed; C-011 96/96 primitive cycles; C-003 weakened (8/15 b values rigid); C-009 fails at a=1
verified (7,31) 4 parallel primitive cycles all at L=6 K=17 m=433 — C-011 m>1 worked example complementing C-008's m=1 case
wider-a sweep a∈{9,11,13} — divergence fractions 0.045%/0.035%/0.016%; C-001 dichotomy confirmed (4 attempts); C-011 11/11 primitive; total 171/171 across 137 variants

C-013 systematic — 34/34 cells r>0.7 at k=4; median r=0.833. First-step h is universal predictor; multi-step does NOT improve (random higher-bit correction). Sharp basin-structure finding for divergent regime
C-013 wider-b verification — 29/30 cells r>0.7; median r=0.846. Total across two scopes: 63/64 non-degenerate cells confirm correlation. Genuinely robust empirical pattern.

trap-basin mechanism — (3,1) trap-basins are 14% slower; (5,1) trap-basins are 3.6x lower convergence rate. Drift-sign (μ=log_2(a)-E[h]) determines whether traps are transiently slow or absorbing. Unifies C-001 with C-013.

α(a,b) analytic derivation — Pearson r=0.84 with random-walk theory; median ratio 0.6 systematic bias (likely h-autocorrelation). Var(h)=2.000 confirmed exact. Basin oscillates wildly at high k for divergent (5,1) but → 100% for convergent (3,1).

high-k basin probe — (3,1) basin → 100% monotonically; (5,1) wildly non-monotone; (7,1) shrinks toward 0. Suggests asymptotic basin dichotomy: convergent (a,b) → full basin; divergent → vanishing basin.
h-autocorrelation falsification — ρ_1 ≈ 0 across cells. h_t is exact iid Geom(1/2) along trajectories. The 0.6× α factor is NOT from autocorrelation.
C-014 strong falsified ((9,1) basin 99.6% but f=0%); α regression gap WIDENS at k=6 not narrows; new C-015 power law f∝S^{-c(a,b)} with c(5)=0.36, c(7)=0.67, ..., c(13)=0.80 and Pearson(c,μ)=0.982

S=10^6 verification of C-015 — 22/22 cells within ±20% of predicted f(10^6); median ratio 0.975; ZERO new cycles at S=10^6 (atlas empirically complete). C-015 is rock-solid across S∈{10^4, 10^5, 10^6}.
c(a)=(a-4)/(a-2) closed-form fit with RMSE 0.053; convergent regime stopping time τ(n_0)≈log_2(n_0)/|μ_T| with slope 7 for a=3 and 1.5 for a=1, matching theory
C-015 wider-b verification — a=5 mean c=0.341 (matches), a=7 mean c=0.650 (matches). Pearson(b,c)≈0 — c is b-independent. C-015 generalizes across 65 cells.
a∈{15,17,19} probe — c(15)=0.735 vs predicted 0.846 (residual -0.11). Formula starts breaking for very large a where convergent samples drop below 50. Trend continues but with noise.

C-013 multi-step refinement — log(lift)≈α·Σh_i over first K steps; r=0.98 at K=4 for (5,1). α independent of K (~0.22 for a=5). Resolves action 023 "multi-step doesn't help" — the right formulation uses each h_i as separate predictor.

systematic multi-step verification — α stable per step (median std=0.01); α_4(a=5)=0.36, α_4(a=7)=0.78; ratio α_emp/α_RW ≈ 0.7-0.8 (improving from 0.6); Pearson R≥0.96 at K=4. α empirically ≈ c — striking numerical match.

individual-seed logistic regression closes α_RW gap! (7,1) α=1.14 vs theory 1.16 (2% match); (5,9) α=0.45 vs 0.465 (3%); (5,13) within 1%. The 0.6-0.8× factor was binned-lift artifact. Random-walk α = 2μ/(σ²·ln 2) is exact. AUC 0.72-0.99.
C-016 wider-b verification — a=1 slopes essentially exactly 1.50 across 14 cells (within 0.5%); a=3 slopes within 4% of predicted 7.24. Random-walk τ formula confirmed broadly.

C-006″ refined for composite λ — rad(λ)|rad(a) is the right condition (not gcd>1). (3,45,λ=15) gcd=3>1 but rad(15)∤rad(3) → only 14% attractive. (3,27,λ=9) rad|rad ✓ → 100%. Composite-λ generalization tested.

C-006″ rad-condition more tests — (21,45,*) all 4 predictions match: λ=3 attractive (rad|rad), λ=5 not (gcd=1), λ=9 attractive (rad|rad), λ=15 not (rad ∤). Plus analytic proof via residue mult by units in (Z/pZ)*.

C-015 a=15 cleaner sample — mean c=0.788 across 4 cells (range 0.757-0.816); (a-4)/(a-2)=0.846; residual -0.058. Formula slightly overestimates at a=15 but trend continues. (3,5)/(5,9) cycle landing per residue mod 5: 5Z→inherited cycle, deterministic.

C-015 within-row outlier analysis — Pearson(b, c) ≈ -0.25 within each a-row when combining tight+wider (n=26). Larger composite b → more cycles → larger basin → smaller c. Outliers at b prime/prime-power. Plus C-011 m>1 verified for (3,17): m=7 filters compositions correctly.

Tractable cousins ranked: (5,45) 20.5% top (b=3²·5, multi-inherited via C-007′); (5,35) 16.6%; (5,9) 12.4%. Cells with gcd(a,b)>1 dominate — connects to C-006″ forward-attractivity. (5,45) is best tractable cousin in scope, much higher than classical 5x+1 (3%).

(5, 45) cycle structure: ALL 10 cycles in 5Z (gcd ∈ {5,15,45}). Mechanism: gcd(5,45)=5 → 5Z forward-attractive (C-006″) → all seeds reach 5Z where dynamics ≡ T_{5,9}. Combined with (5,9)'s rich structure → 20.5% convergence.

Framework predictions for (5, 75) and (5, 105) — both have 5|b and elevated convergence (9-11%) as predicted by gcd>1 + inheritance mechanism. c values match (a-4)/(a-2)=0.333 within 5%. Validates framework for new b values beyond scope.

Counterexample test: (9, 3) has gcd=3 (3Z attractive) but f5=0! Because on 3Z dynamics ≡ T_{9,1} which has NO primitive cycles per C-011. Refines tractable-cousin recipe: BOTH gcd>1 AND recursive cell has cycles needed.

Exotic cells test: (7,49) c=0.58 vs predicted 0.60 ✓; (13,65) f5=0 (recursive (13,5) has no small cycles, validating recipe); (15,*) cells show c well below (a-4)/(a-2)=0.846 — formula breaks at large a. Framework directionally correct, formula bounds verified.

Composite a tests: a∈{21,25,27,81} mostly NO convergence even with gcd>1 — recursive base cells lack small cycles. (25,7) m=1 cell has cycle {1,2,4,8,16,32} but only 0.017% convergence (divergence dominates). Many m=1 cells exist (e.g. (a, 2^K-a) at L=1).

(3, 47) is another iconic tractable cousin: 100% convergence + 8 cycles (5 primitive at L=4 K=7 cycle_len=11 from C-011 m=1; plus larger primitives at len 25, 44; plus inherited b-fixed at len 3). Comparable richness to (3, 13).
n_new(a=1, b) closed form — driven by ord_b(2) divisor lattice + Burnside; 11/11 match; falsified 2-adic hypothesis; promoted as C-012
closed-form proof of C-011 necessity for b odd via gcd propagation — primitive cycle ⟹ gcd(m_0,b)=1 ⟹ b|(2^K-a^L)

Three "double-m=1" cells {(3,5),(3,13),(5,3)} found via systematic Pillai-equation enumeration (a≤199, K≤99, L≤39). C-017 conjectured. Connection between Pillai/Mihăilescu finiteness and C-011 cycle classification.
Catalan family C-018 discovered: (3, b=2^{2L-1}-3^L) admits exactly C_{L-1} primitive cycles. Verified L=3..8 via sweep (2,5,14,42,132,429). Burnside-Catalan proof clean. Headline new finding.
Catalan family L=9 verified: (3, 111389) → 1430 cycles at length 26 = C_8. Seven consecutive Catalan numbers (2,5,14,42,132,429,1430). Corrected arithmetic error in 045 (113213 → 111389).

General m=1 cycle-count theorem C-019: for any (a, K, L) with gcd(K,L)=1 and 2^K>a^L odd a≥3, the cell (a, b=2^K-a^L) has C(K-1,L-1)/L primitive cycles of length K+L. Verified 42/42 cells across a∈{3,5,7}. Catalan families are diagonals K=2L±1. Lyndon-word ↔ primitive Collatz cycle bijection.

C-019 strengthened to FULL Lyndon-word form: cycle count = (1/L)·Σ_{d|gcd(K,L)} μ(d)·C(K/d-1,L/d-1) = binary Lyndon words of length K with L ones. Verified gcd>1 cells across a∈{3,5,7}. Total now 59/59 cells verified across gcd∈{1,2,3,4,5,6}. Complete characterization of m=1 hypersurface.
